title: Continuation of the National Emergency With Respect to Iran
summary: The President has continued the national emergency with respect to Iran for another year, as declared on March 15, 1995, by Executive Order 12957. This action is taken because the actions and policies of the Iranian government, including its weapons proliferation, regional aggression, support for terrorism, and the activities of the Islamic Revolutionary Guard Corps, continue to pose a significant threat to U.S. national security, foreign policy, and economy. This emergency declaration is separate from the one related to the 1979 hostage crisis.
